best-of-N sampling verifier language models paper

action.query: Training verifiers to solve math problems Cobbe 2021 arXiv

output.revised_prompt:
Create a clean, NeurIPS-style vector diagram (single slide/figure) that summarizes the pipeline of “G-BoN: Out-of-Distribution-Gated Best-of-N for Robust, Plug-In Test-Time Verification of Vision-Language-Action Policies.” The figure must be simple but illustrative, occupy about one-third of a page when placed in a paper, and exclude conclusions, limitations, and preliminaries.

Overall style
- White background, black/gray lines, subtle accent colors only (green for the gate/pass path, blue for BoN verification path). 
- Sans-serif font similar to Helvetica/CMU Sans, 9–10 pt at final size. 
- Consistent stroke width; minimal icons; no heavy gradients; compact spacing suitable for a two-column NeurIPS preprint.
- Output as crisp vector artwork.

Layout (left-to-right main flow with two small dashed training insets along the bottom):

1) Inputs (left block)
- Title: “Inputs”
- Inside: two small sub-icons/labels side-by-side:
  • Camera frames o_t (tiny camera icon or stacked frames) 
  • Instruction ℓ (small text bubble icon)
- Rightward arrow to next block.

2) Frozen VLA backbone π_θ (center-left block)
- Title bar: “Frozen VLA π_θ (no retraining)”
- Inside, show two outputs with labels:
  • Greedy action chunk â_t ∈ R^{K×D_a}
  • Mean action-query hidden state z_t ∈ R^D
- From this block, draw two thin arrows: one to the OOD gate (diamond) and one directly to the “Trust policy” output (to be used only when the gate passes). The direct path should be grey and annotated later by the gate decision.

3) OOD gate (diamond, centered)
- Diamond node labeled: “OOD head g_ϕ(z_t) → s = σ(g_ϕ(z_t))”
- Beneath the diamond, show a small threshold label: “compare to τ* (Youden-J calibrated)”
- Two outgoing branches:
  • Green pass branch (condition: s ≤ τ*): label on the arrow “in-distribution → trust policy”; connect directly to the final output (Execute â_t). 
  • Blue verify branch (condition: s > τ*): label on the arrow “out-of-distribution → verify (BoN)”; route to the candidate generator block.

4) Candidate generator (right of gate; blue path)
- Small block labeled: “Sample N candidates around â_t:  a^(1..N) = â_t + σ·ε”
- Rightward arrow to Verifier.

5) Verifier with frozen features (to the right; blue)
- Main block title: “Frozen-feature cross-attention scorer V(z_t, a^(i), M_topk)”
- To its lower-left, include a tiny memory icon/block labeled “Memory bank M: {(z_m, a_m)}; retrieve top-k” with a thin dashed connector into V to indicate optional retrieval context.
- Inside V, add a small list/label: “score each candidate → argmax_i score”
- Rightward arrow from V to the final output labeled: “Select a^(i*)”.

6) Final output (far right)
- Block labeled: “Execute chosen action chunk”
- Two incoming arrows merge here: the green pass branch carrying â_t and the blue verified branch carrying a^(i*). Use a small merge symbol or junction to emphasize the mixture-of-experts routing.

Bottom training insets (dashed boxes, small, unobtrusive):
A) “OOD head training” (under the gate)
- Two mini columns within the dashed box:
  • Left: “Clean first-frame z_t (LIBERO)” with a small stack-of-cards icon.
  • Right: “Perturbed first-frame z_t (LIBERO-Plus)” with a tiny ‘spark’/noise icon.
- Footer line: “Train 2-layer MLP with BCE; select τ* via Youden’s J on val set.”

B) “Verifier training” (under the V block)
- Text: “Build memory bank from demos; train V with listwise softmax + margin-rank over {GT, policy, noise} candidates. Frozen backbone.”

Legend/notes (tiny, bottom-left corner, gray text):
- “Plug-in test-time procedure; backbone frozen.”
- “Gate fires only when OOD score high; otherwise trust greedy policy.”

Figure title (very small, top-left):
- “G-BoN: OOD-gated Best-of-N at test time”

References to inspire visual style and concepts (do not copy figures; just align with their level of abstraction; show as a tiny gray footnote inside the figure):
- OpenVLA: An Open-Source Vision-Language-Action Model (arXiv:2406.09246)
- LIBERO benchmark (NeurIPS 2023)
- Self-Consistency for LLMs (arXiv:2203.11171)
- Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems (arXiv:2110.14168)
- SelectiveNet: Integrated Reject Option (ICML 2019)

Ensure:
- Clean, compact, single-panel layout that visually reads left→right.
- Clear math labels (o_t, ℓ, z_t, g_ϕ, τ*, a^(i), V, M_topk).
- No sections on conclusion/limitations/preliminaries.
- The entire design should naturally fit one-third of a NeurIPS page height as a banner figure.
